expectations.text: Uses useWriteContracts hook (not useSendCalls or custom encoding)
expectations.evidence: import { useWriteContracts } from 'wagmi/experimental' — correct high-level EIP-5792 hook that handles ABI encoding automatically

expectations.text: Uses useCapabilities for wallet EIP-5792 support detection
expectations.evidence: import { useCapabilities } from 'wagmi/experimental' — detects wallet capabilities and checks for EIP-5792 support

expectations.text: Uses useShowCallsStatus for batch transaction status display
expectations.evidence: import { useShowCallsStatus } from 'wagmi/experimental' — provides showCallsStatusAsync to display batch status in wallet UI

expectations.text: Provides graceful fallback for wallets without EIP-5792 support
expectations.evidence: Has both 'Batch: Approve + Transfer (EIP-5792)' button AND 'Individual: Approve, then Transfer (2 txns)' fallback button with divider

expectations.text: Batch button conditionally disabled when wallet doesn't support EIP-5792
ERC20 smart contract with approve+transfer pattern created
expectations.evidence: BatchToken.sol with approve(), transferWithTracking(), mint() functions and TransferTracked event

expectations.text: Frontend page with batch UI created
expectations.evidence: /batch-tokens page with wallet capabilities card, token info, transfer form, batch status

expectations.text: Uses SE-2 scaffold hooks for contract interaction
expectations.evidence: useScaffoldReadContract for balanceOf/name/symbol/decimals/allowance, useScaffoldWriteContract for fallback, useDeployedContractInfo for batch

expectations.text: No new npm dependencies needed (wagmi already has EIP-5792 hooks)
expectations.evidence: No dependencies added to package.json — wagmi 2.19.5 already ships experimental EIP-5792 hooks
